skill.name: mmx-cli
skill.description: Use mmx to generate text, images, video, speech, and music via the MiniMax AI platform. Use when the user wants to create media content, chat with MiniMax models, perform web search, or manage MiniMax API resources from the terminal.
skill.summary: Terminal CLI for multimodal AI generation with MiniMax: text, images, video, speech, and music.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported 202 potential issues, all of which are false positives. SKILL.md is a markdown documentation file for the mmx CLI tool. The scanner misidentified markdown code block backticks as Ruby/shell backtick execution, model version strings as weak cryptographic algorithms, and CLI command examples as system reconnaissance. The skill instructs an AI agent to run the mmx CLI with user-provided parameters, which involves legitimate external command execution, filesystem access for credentials (~/.mmx/), and environment variable usage for API keys. No malicious intent, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration patterns were found.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI agent to execute the mmx CLI tool with user-provided parameters. This is the core functionality of the skill and is expected behavior for a CLI helper. Arguments are constructed from user input but passed through the CLI tool interface, not directly to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is entirely documentation for a CLI tool. Command execution is the intended purpose, not a vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Credential File Storage
low_findings.description: The CLI stores API credentials in ~/.mmx/credentials.json. This is a standard pattern for CLI tools but represents filesystem access with sensitive data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard CLI credential storage pattern, similar to ~/.gitconfig or ~/.ssh/. Not malicious but involves sensitive file access.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable API Key Access
low_findings.description: The skill documents using MINIMAX_API_KEY environment variable for authentication. Standard pattern for API-based CLI tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard environment variable pattern for API keys, documented in the configuration section.

content.user_title: Generate AI media from the terminal
content.value_statement: Create text, images, video, speech, and music using the MiniMax AI platform directly from your terminal. Avoid complex API integration by using simple command-line commands for all your AI generation needs.
actual_capabilities: Generate text completions and multi-turn conversations via MiniMax models
Create images from text prompts with configurable aspect ratios and batch counts
Generate video content with async task workflows and webhook callbacks
Synthesize speech with customizable voice, speed, pitch, and format options
Produce music with detailed style, genre, lyrical, and structural control
Perform web search through the MiniMax platform
limitations: Requires a MiniMax API key and active account for all operations
Video generation tasks are asynchronous and may take significant time to complete
Speech synthesis is limited to 10,000 characters per request
Music and video outputs depend on MiniMax service availability and regional access

use_cases.title: Content creation workflow
use_cases.description: Content creators generate images, video, speech, and music directly from terminal scripts without switching to web interfaces or writing API integration code.

use_cases.title: AI prototyping and development
use_cases.description: Developers quickly test MiniMax model outputs and integrate generation features into applications using the CLI as a prototyping tool.

use_cases.title: Automated media production
use_cases.description: Teams automate media generation pipelines by chaining mmx commands with standard Unix tools like jq for JSON processing.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a cat sitting on a cloud at sunset. Save it to the current directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image generation for a project

prompt_templates.title: Multi-turn text conversation
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a conversation with MiniMax using a system prompt that sets it as a Python tutor. Then ask it to explain list comprehensions with examples.

prompt_templates.title: Async video generation workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short video of ocean waves. Use async mode so I can continue working. Show me the task ID, then check its status and download when complete.
prompt_templates.scenario: Background video generation with status tracking

prompt_templates.title: Chain image generation with vision analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a sunset. Then use vision describe to analyze the generated image and describe what it contains. Pipe the results through jq to extract only the description text.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced pipeline combining multiple mmx commands

output_examples.input: mmx text chat --message "user:What is the capital of France?" --output json --quiet
output_examples.output: The AI responds with the answer in JSON format, containing the response text and token usage information.

output_examples.input: mmx image generate --prompt "A cat in a spacesuit" --output json --quiet
output_examples.output: The command returns one or more image URLs pointing to the generated images. In quiet mode, URLs appear one per line on stdout.
The command creates an MP3 audio file at the specified path. The file path is printed to stdout upon completion.

best_practices: Always use --non-interactive and --quiet flags in automated or CI environments to prevent unexpected prompts and progress output.
Use --output json for machine-readable results when piping command output to other tools or scripts.
Set your MINIMAX_API_KEY as an environment variable rather than passing it as a flag in each command to avoid exposing credentials in shell history.
anti_patterns: Do not pass API keys directly in command flags for shared or logged environments. Use environment variables or the mmx auth login command instead.
Do not use the CLI in interactive mode within automated scripts. Interactive prompts will block execution and cause timeouts.
Do not ignore the --async flag for video generation in production workflows. Synchronous video generation can block for extended periods.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with the MiniMax API?
faq.answer: Run mmx auth login --api-key with your API key. Credentials are stored in ~/.mmx/credentials.json. You can also set the MINIMAX_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Which MiniMax models are supported?
faq.answer: The CLI supports MiniMax-M2.7 for text, image-01 for images, MiniMax-Hailuo-2.3 for video, speech-2.8-hd for speech, and music-2.5 for music generation.

faq.question: How do I use mmx in automated scripts or CI pipelines?
faq.answer: Use the --non-interactive flag to fail fast on missing arguments, --quiet to suppress progress output, and --output json for machine-readable results.

faq.question: Can I pipe the output of one mmx command into another?
faq.answer: Yes. stdout outputs clean data that can be piped. For example, pipe image generation output into vision describe, or use command substitution to capture task IDs.

faq.question: How does async video generation work?
faq.answer: Use --async to start a task and receive a task ID. Poll the task status with mmx video task get, then download the result with mmx video download when complete.

faq.question: What regions does the CLI support?
faq.answer: The CLI auto-detects your region. You can override with --region global or --region cn. Set it persistently with mmx config set --key region.
